title: Zoologists and Wildlife Biologists
description: Study the origins, behavior, diseases, genetics, and life processes of animals and wildlife. May specialize in wildlife research and management. May collect and analyze biological data to determine the environmental effects of present and potential use of land and water habitats.

tasks.text: Develop, or make recommendations on, management systems and plans for wildlife populations and habitat, consulting with stakeholders and the public at large to explore options.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Inventory or estimate plant and wildlife populations.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Inform and respond to public regarding wildlife and conservation issues, such as plant identification, hunting ordinances, and nuisance wildlife.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Study animals in their natural habitats, assessing effects of environment and industry on animals, interpreting findings and recommending alternative operating conditions for industry.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Disseminate information by writing reports and scientific papers or journal articles, and by making presentations and giving talks for schools, clubs, interest groups and park interpretive programs.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Study characteristics of animals, such as origin, interrelationships, classification, life histories, diseases, development, genetics, and distribution.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Perform administrative duties, such as fundraising, public relations, budgeting, and supervision of zoo staff.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Check for, and ensure compliance with, environmental laws, and notify law enforcement when violations are identified.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Analyze characteristics of animals to identify and classify them.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.
Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Organize and conduct experimental studies with live animals in controlled or natural surroundings.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Coordinate preventive programs to control the outbreak of wildlife diseases.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Prepare collections of preserved specimens or microscopic slides for species identification and study of development or disease.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Collect and dissect animal specimens and examine specimens under microscope.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
